records.ety: [OE. stede place, AS. stede; akin to LG. & D. stede, OS. stad, stedi, OHG. stat, G. statt, stätte, Icel. staðr, Dan. sted, Sw. stad, Goth. staþs, and E. stand. √163. See Stand, and cf. Staith, Stithy.]

quotes: Fly, therefore, fly this fearful stead anon.

defs: Place or room which another had, has, or might have.
quotes: In stead of bounds, he a pillar set.

defs: A frame on which a bed is laid; a bedstead.
quotes: The genial bed, Sallow the feet, the borders, and the stead.
The smallest act . . . shall stand us in great stead.
Here thy sword can do thee little stead.
notes: ☞ The word is now commonly used as the last part of a compound; as, farmstead, homestead, roadstead, etc.

defs: To help; to support; to benefit; to assist.
quotes: Perhaps my succour or advisement meet, Mote stead you much your purpose to subdue.
It nothing steads us To chide him from our eaves.
